Approach 1: Airbus Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of the cash a company may generate in the future and discounts each year back to today, to arrive at an estimate of what the entire business could be worth right now.

For Airbus, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about €3.8b. Analysts provide detailed forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extends those estimates further, giving projected free cash flow of €11.0b by 2030. The values between those years reflect both analyst inputs and extrapolated figures.

Bringing all of these projected cash flows back to today and adding them up produces an estimated intrinsic value of about €282.29 per share. Compared with the recent share price of €215.10, this implies a 23.8% discount, which points to the shares trading below this DCF estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Airbus Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like Airbus, the P/E ratio is a common way to think about value, because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is currently generating. Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk often point to a lower, more cautious multiple.

Airbus currently trades on a P/E of 33.5x. That sits below the Aerospace & Defense industry average of about 51.5x and also below the peer average of 38.9x. On the surface, that suggests the market is applying a lower multiple than many comparable names in the sector.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Airbus is 34.9x, which is its view of what a reasonable P/E could be given the company’s earnings growth profile, profit margins, industry, market value and risk factors. This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for Airbus’ specific characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. With the current P/E at 33.5x versus a Fair Ratio of 34.9x, Airbus shares screen as slightly undervalued on this metric.
